channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Air Canada and Union Reach Tentative Agreement on Technical and Logistics Staff
item.description: Air Canada has reached a tentative collective agreement with the International Association of Machinists and Aerospace Workers (IAMAW) union, which represents approximately 11,000 technical, logistics...

item.title: Murang'a Woman Convicted of Manslaughter After Husband's Death
item.description: Joyce Mwangi, a mother of three from Murang'a County, was jailed for seven years after her husband died in a violent altercation. The court convicted her of manslaughter in June 2026 but ruled her sev...

item.title: Andy Burnham's Impact on English Football Reforms and Governance
item.description: The article explores Andy Burnham's significant contributions to English football governance, highlighting his role in shaping key reforms. Burnham, a Labour politician, has been instrumental in initi...

item.title: Punjab leader criticizes OTT platform for removing film highlighting state-sanctioned killings
item.description: Sukhbir Singh Badal, president of the Shiromani Akali Dal (SAD), has strongly condemned the removal of Diljit Dosanjh's film 'Satluj' from ZEE5, calling it an assault on Punjab's collective memory and...

item.title: President Samia Highlights Tanzania's Commitment to Human Rights
item.description: President Samia Suluhu has emphasized Tanzania's dedication to advancing human rights, highlighting progress made while acknowledging ongoing challenges. During a speech at the African Union's Inter-P...

item.title: Content Creators Capture Mysterious Sky Object in Backgrounds
item.description: A mysterious object streaking across the sky in the backgrounds of content creators has sparked widespread speculation on social media. Initially mistaken for a meteor or shooting star, the object's u...

item.title: UK triple-murder suspect to face firearm trial in South Africa before extradition
item.description: Ndodana Mkhanyisi Tshuma, a 45-year-old British citizen of Zimbabwean heritage, is set to face trial in South Africa for unlawful possession of an unlicensed 9mm firearm before his extradition to the ...

item.title: Trump praises would-be assassin's academic achievements after 2024 assassination attempt
item.description: Former U.S. President Donald Trump delivered an unusual and contradictory statement on Fox & Friends, discussing the 2024 assassination attempt by Thomas Crooks. While criticizing the Secret Service's...

item.title: Tanzania achieves 85.2% growth in clean water access for 10.2 million people
item.description: Tanzania has made significant progress in improving access to clean water services, with an 85.2% increase benefiting over 10.2 million citizens. This development is part of broader efforts to enhance...

item.title: Ted Lasso star Cristo Fernandez debuts in USL Championship with El Paso Locomotive
item.description: Mexican actor Cristo Fernandez, best known for his role as Dani Rojas in the AppleTV+ series Ted Lasso, has made his professional football debut with American second division side El Paso Locomotive. ...

item.title: PSX declines amid Middle East tensions and global market volatility
item.description: The Pakistan Stock Exchange (PSX) opened the week with significant losses, driven by escalating hostilities in the Middle East. The KSE-100 index fell over 2,300 points on Monday, hitting an intraday ...

item.title: Quebec Innu community rejects $2.5 billion hydro deal in referendum
item.description: Members of the Pessamit Innu First Nation, located northeast of Quebec City, have rejected a proposed $2.5 billion hydro development agreement with Hydro-Québec and the provincial government. The refe...

item.title: Migori MCA Urges President William Ruto to Extend Presidency Beyond Term Limits
item.description: A Migori County Assembly member, George Omamba, has publicly called for President William Ruto to extend his term beyond the constitutional two-term limit. During a meeting at State House Nairobi with...

item.title: Somali Migrants Choose to Remain in South Africa Amid Anti-Immigrant Protests
item.description: The article highlights the decision of Somali migrants to stay in South Africa despite growing anti-immigrant protests and government crackdowns. Amir Sheikh, chairperson of the African Diaspora Forum...

item.title: ERC Clarifies Consumers Don't Need to Pay Full Disputed Electricity Bills
item.description: The Energy Regulatory Commission (ERC) has clarified that consumers disputing electricity bills do not need to pay the full contested amount immediately, addressing concerns about unfair billing pract...

item.title: Former Tory Minister Ann Widdecombe Found Dead in Murder Case
item.description: Ann Widdecombe, a former Conservative minister and prominent political figure, was found dead at her home in Dartmoor, Devon, following a sudden disappearance. Police confirmed her death as murder, wi...

item.title: Cadaver Fat Injectable Gains Attention as GLP-1 Weight Loss Drives Demand for Body Contouring
item.description: A new injectable body contouring product called alloClae is attracting growing attention in the U.S. because it is made from donated human fat obtained from cadaver tissue. Developed by Tiger Aestheti...
